articles.title: Weather Update: Heavy Rain Alert In Uttarakhand, Himachal; Heatwave Sweeps UP, MP | State-Wise Forecast - The Daily Jagran
articles.search_term: heatwave (Bilaspur OR Chamba OR Hamirpur OR Kangra OR Kinnaur OR Kullu OR "Lahaul and Spiti" OR Mandi OR Shimla OR "Sirmaur (Sirmour)" OR Solan OR Una) Himachal Pradesh
articles.full_text:
The IMD said the maximum temperatures in Delhi are likely to rise to 39 degrees Celsius, while the minimum may stay close to 28 degrees Celsius. In the evening, it will experience cloudy skies, accompanied by winds around 20 to 30 kmph.
Several districts in Uttar Pradesh, including Agra, Mathura, Kanpur, Varanasi, Mirzapur, and Ballia, have been placed under rain and storm alert. Winds are likely to blow at speeds of up to 65 kmph. Lucknow may witness a maximum temperature of 40 degrees Celsius and a minimum of 28 degrees Celsius on Friday. Places such Noida and Greater Noida may experience a heatwave.

Uttarakhand, Himachal Pradesh And Punjab To Receive Heavy Rain
Several districts across Uttarakhand, including Rudraprayag, Tehri Garhwal, Udham Singh Nagar and Bageshwar may receive heavy rain and thunderstorms. Strong winds with speeds up to 70 kmph may accompany the weather activity. Dehradun is likely to witness temperatures between 22 degrees Celsius and 34 degrees Celsius on Friday (May 16).
Himachal Pradesh is expected to receive heavy rainfall and storms in districts such as Kangra, Chamba, Shimla and Solan. Strong winds with speeds of 70-80 kmph are also likely during this period. In Manali, temperatures on May 16 may range between 2 degrees Celsius and 11 degrees Celsius.
Rain and thunderstorm alerts have been issued for many parts of Punjab, including Amritsar, Mohali, Moga and Ludhiana. Winds are expected to blow at speeds of 50 to 60 kmph during this period.
Rajasthan's several districts such as Alwar, Jodhpur, Ajmer and Udaipur may receive heavy rainfall. Strong winds with speeds of 50-60 kmph are also likely during this period. In Jaipur, temperatures on May 16 may range between 29 degrees Celsius and 41 degrees Celsius.

Heatwave conditions in Madhya Pradesh may worsen in districts such as Dhar, Vidisha, Rewa and Singrauli. Winds with speeds of 20-30 kmph are also expected. In Bhopal, temperatures on May 16 may range between 28 degrees Celsius and 41 degrees Celsius.

articles.title: Heat to intensify in Himachal from tomorrow: Temperature may rise by 5°C; yellow alert in 4 districts today - Bhaskar English
articles.search_term: heatwave (Bilaspur OR Chamba OR Hamirpur OR Kangra OR Kinnaur OR Kullu OR "Lahaul and Spiti" OR Mandi OR Shimla OR "Sirmaur (Sirmour)" OR Solan OR Una) Himachal Pradesh
articles.full_text:
Heat to intensify in Himachal from tomorrow:Temperature may rise by 5°C; yellow alert in 4 districts today
The intensity of heat will now increase in Himachal Pradesh. After light drizzle in some parts during the last five days, sunshine will return from tomorrow. According to the Meteorological Department, the weather will remain completely clear till May 21. During this period, the temperature will rise by four to five degrees.
The effect of heat has started to be visible in the plains of the state. Una's maximum temperature has reached 37 degrees Celsius. However, it is still 1.7 degrees below normal. Due to cloudy skies and light rain in some areas during the last four days, the temperature in many cities remained below normal, but now with clear weather, it will rise rapidly.
Storm warning in 4 districts today
The Meteorological Department has predicted light rain in some areas today. The department has issued a yellow alert for thunderstorms in Chamba, Kangra, Kullu, and Mandi districts. However, alerts have been continuously issued for the past five days. Specifically, May 12 and 13 had an orange alert for heavy hailstorms and thunderstorms. But even during this period, most parts remained sunny.
13 percent more rain in May than normal
According to the Meteorological Department, 13 percent more rainfall has been recorded in May so far than normal. This is why people in the state did not feel the heat even until mid-May this time. However, with continuous sunshine in the coming days, temperatures will start to rise in the plains and mid-altitude regions.
